story.title: Dinghy v0.3: Electron desktop app with config.yml file I/O and FFmpeg RTSP frame grab, rebranded from Frigate Config Builder to Dinghy
story.as_a: Frigate user running a headless server or NVR box
story.i_want: a native desktop app (named Dinghy) that opens my real config.yml, pulls a still frame straight from my camera's RTSP stream, and saves YAML back to disk
story.so_that: I configure zones end-to-end without screenshots, copy-paste, or hosting anything

acceptance_criteria: Given the repo, when `npm start` runs, then the existing single-file app opens in a BrowserWindow titled 'Dinghy' with dinghy.png as window/dock icon, contextIsolation on, nodeIntegration off, and all v0.2 features working unchanged

Given File → Open Config… (or the in-app Import 'From file…' button under Electron), when the user picks a config.yml, then its content runs through the existing Importer flow and the chosen path is remembered for saving
Given File → Save Config / Save Config As…, when invoked, then the 'All cameras' full config YAML is written to the remembered/chosen path and a toast confirms the write

Given a camera with an RTSP input URL, when the user clicks 'Grab Frame' in the editor, then the main process spawns ffmpeg to capture one frame and the image loads as that camera's canvas background via the existing Store.setImage path
Given ffmpeg is not installed or the stream is unreachable, when Grab Frame runs, then a clear inline error (toast) explains the cause and nothing crashes; a 15s timeout guards hung streams
Given the same HTML file opened directly in a browser (no Electron), when used, then everything works exactly as v0.2 — native-only UI (Grab Frame button) is hidden and the logo image fails soft
Given `npm run dist`, when packaging completes, then electron-builder produces platform installers (dmg/AppImage/exe per host OS) with Dinghy branding and no network access required at runtime
constraints: Renderer remains the single HTML file (renamed dinghy.html, git mv to preserve history); no bundler, no framework; all native access via a preload contextBridge API window.dinghyNative
Main process and preload are plain JavaScript (no TypeScript build step), CommonJS, in src/
IPC surface (the v0.3 contract): openConfig() → {path, content}|null; saveConfig(content, {as}) → {path}|error; grabFrame(rtspUrl) → {dataUrl}|{error}; menu events forwarded via onMenu(cb)

FFmpeg invocation: spawn('ffmpeg', ['-rtsp_transport','tcp','-i',url,'-frames:v','1','-f','image2pipe','-vcodec','mjpeg','-']) capturing stdout, 15s kill timeout; URL passed as argv (never shell-interpolated)
Renderer must keep working with zero native code present: every dinghyNative use guarded by existence check

Save writes the generated full config (Yaml.fullConfig); merging into an existing file's untouched keys is explicitly out of scope for v0.3 (master spec allows deferral) — Save Config As is the safe default when a foreign config was opened

Branding: app/product name 'Dinghy', tagline 'gets you into Frigate'; header shows dinghy.png logo with onerror fallback; HTML title 'Dinghy'; package.json name dinghy; electron-builder productName Dinghy, icon from build/icon.png (copy of dinghy.png)
No telemetry, no auto-update, no network calls from main process
edge_cases: Grab Frame with credentials in RTSP URL — URL never logged or echoed in errors (strip before reporting)
Save Config when no path is remembered — falls back to Save As dialog
Open Config canceled in dialog — no-op, no error
ffmpeg writes warnings to stderr but succeeds — stdout image still used; stderr only surfaced on nonzero exit
Window closed during a pending grab — child process killed
Browser context: paste/copy/download continue using web Platform paths untouched
dependencies: v0.2 complete (Platform adapter boundary, Importer.parse pure)
ffmpeg on user PATH at runtime (graceful absence)
risks: Medium: ffmpeg not installed on user machines — mitigated by explicit error with install hint; bundling ffmpeg deferred to keep installers small and licensing simple
Low: single generated-config save model may surprise users expecting in-place merge — Save As default + README note
Low: electron sandbox/test limitations in CI-less repo — verified by syntax checks + browser parity run; full GUI smoke on user machine

open_questions.question: Bundle an ffmpeg binary or require system ffmpeg?

options.label: System ffmpeg with graceful error (Recommended)
options.info: Keeps installers ~100MB smaller, avoids GPL distribution questions; NVR users typically have ffmpeg.

options.label: Bundle ffmpeg-static
options.info: Zero-setup grabs but +60-100MB per platform and license diligence.

tasks.description: Rebrand: git mv to dinghy.html, title/header/logo updates, serve.js + README updates

Electron scaffold: package.json, src/main.js (BrowserWindow, menu, IPC handlers incl. ffmpeg spawn with timeout+credential-stripping), src/preload.js (contextBridge), electron-builder.yml, build/icon.png
Renderer native integration (guarded): Grab Frame button per active camera, menu-event handlers for open/save/export wired to Importer/Yaml/Toast
QA: browser parity run (v0.2 regression in preview), node --check on main/preload, IPC contract review, ffmpeg-missing error path, packaging config sanity
Human: full GUI smoke on macOS (npm start), real RTSP grab, npm run dist

docs_md:
## Dinghy v0.3 — Electron
The v0.2 web app becomes a desktop app. Renderer is unchanged dinghy.html loaded in a BrowserWindow; native powers arrive through a 4-method preload bridge: open config.yml (feeds the existing importer), save generated config, grab a single RTSP frame via system ffmpeg, and menu forwarding. Opening dinghy.html in a plain browser still works exactly like v0.2.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Entire native surface — the v0.3 contract

metrics: Browser parity: full v0.2 QA scenario suite passes on renamed dinghy.html with 0 regressions
node --check passes on src/main.js and src/preload.js; 0 nodeIntegration/remote usage; contextIsolation true
Grab-frame error path returns structured {error} (no throw, no credential leakage) when ffmpeg is absent — verified on this machine which lacks ffmpeg
npm run dist completes producing a Dinghy-branded artifact (human-verified, T5)

decision_log.rationale: The v0.2 Platform boundary was built for exactly this: native capability lands as an additive, guarded bridge rather than a fork of the app. One consolidated spec (not 3) because the Electron shell, file I/O, and frame grab share one IPC surface and ship together or not at all; rebrand rides along since it touches the same files.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: Tauri — smaller binaries but Rust toolchain contradicts the boring-stack philosophy and master spec names Electron
Rejected: TypeScript main process — optional per master spec; would add the project's first build step
Rejected: in-place YAML merge on save — high corruption risk without round-trip-preserving parser; deferred with Save As as safe default
tradeoffs: Save writes generated config, not a merge into the original file
GUI smoke + packaging verification falls to the human (T5) given sandboxed dev environment
decision_log.confidence_rationale: IPC/Electron patterns are standard and the renderer boundary was pre-built; residual risk is environment-specific (ffmpeg variance, packaging quirks) which T5 covers.

defaults_applied: Assuming rename to dinghy.html is acceptable as part of rebrand (web version path changes)
Assuming tagline 'gets you into Frigate' from user's phrasing
Assuming electron-builder targets host-OS artifact in v0.3 (cross-OS matrix deferred to CI later)
